tool_name: Steam Boiler Thermal Efficiency — Indirect Loss Method, Flue Gas Dew Point & Stack Heat Loss

inputs.name: Fuel Type

options.label: Natural gas — LHV=47500 kJ/kg, C=75%, H=24%, S=0%
Fuel oil No.6 HFO — LHV=40600 kJ/kg, C=86%, H=11%, S=2.5%
LPG Propane — LHV=46350 kJ/kg, C=82%, H=18%, S=0%
Coal (bituminous) — LHV=26000 kJ/kg, C=73%, H=5%, S=1.5%

inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Flue Gas O₂ Concentration (dry basis)
inputs.note: Measured by portable O₂ analyser; natural gas target: 2–3% O₂

inputs.name: Flue Gas CO Concentration (dry basis)
inputs.note: < 100 ppm: good combustion; > 400 ppm: incomplete combustion — EN 303-5 limit: 250 ppm

inputs.name: Surface Radiation & Convection Loss (% of fired duty)
inputs.note: ASME PTC 4 Appendix B: well-insulated gas boiler ≈ 0.3–0.8%; older/larger ≈ 0.5–1.5%
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT5 — Precision grinding tolerance: 4-18µm (≈0.5% for Ø10-50mm)

formulas: XS_air_pct = flue_gas_O2_pct * 100 / (21 - flue_gas_O2_pct)  // [%] — Excess Air Percentage (XS_air) — Ref: XS_air = O₂% × 100 / (21 − O₂%) — ASME PTC 4-2013 §5.3 Eq.(5.2)

const FC = {NG:{LHV:47500,C:0.75,H:0.24,S:0}, HFO:{LHV:40600,C:0.86,H:0.11,S:0.025}, LPG:{LHV:46350,C:0.82,H:0.18,S:0}, Coal:{LHV:26000,C:0.73,H:0.05,S:0.015}}; const fp = FC[fuel_type]; LHV_kj_kg=fp.LHV; C_frac=fp.C; H_frac=fp.H; S_frac=fp.S  // [kJ/kg] — Fuel Composition Parameters by Type — Ref: Fuel composition from EN 12952-15 Table A.1 / ASME PTC 4 Appendix C

m_air_stoich = 11.5 * C_frac + 34.5 * H_frac + 4.3 * S_frac; m_fg_kg_kg = m_air_stoich * (1 + XS_air_pct/100) + 1; cp_fg = 1.004 + 0.000254 * stack_temp_c; L_fg_pct = m_fg_kg_kg * cp_fg * (stack_temp_c - ambient_temp_c) / LHV_kj_kg * 100  // [%] — Dry Flue Gas Heat Loss (L_fg) — Ref: L_DFG = m_fg × c_p,fg × ΔT / LHV × 100 — ASME PTC 4 §5.4 Eq.(5.11)

L_CO_pct = CO_stack_ppm * 3.5e-4 * (XS_air_pct + 100) / 100  // [%] — Incomplete Combustion Loss — CO (L_CO) — Ref: L_CO ≈ CO_ppm × 3.5×10⁻⁴ × (1 + XS/100) — simplified Ostwald formula; ASME PTC 4 §5.8
eta_th_pct = 100 - L_fg_pct - L_CO_pct - radiation_loss_pct  // [%] — Net Thermal Efficiency (η_th) — Ref: η = 100 − ΣLosses (indirect method) — EN 12952-15 §7.3; ASME PTC 4 §5.1

T_dew_c = (fuel_type === 'HFO' || fuel_type === 'Coal') ? 120 + 18 * Math.log10(S_frac * 1000) : 55  // [°C] — Flue Gas Acid Dew Point Temperature (T_dew) — Ref: Müller-Kirchenbauer dew point: T_dew ≈ 120 + 18·log(SO₃ ppm) for S-bearing fuels; H₂SO₄ formation — EN 15502-1 §B.4

rules.message: Boiler efficiency < 75%: critically low — stack temperature and/or excess air are excessive; immediate combustion optimisation and flue gas audit required
CO > 400 ppm: incomplete combustion — adjust air-to-fuel ratio; CO > 1000 ppm indicates safety risk of CO accumulation in boiler room
Stack temperature within 10°C of acid dew point: sulphuric acid condensation risk — economiser and stack lining corrosion; maintain stack temperature ≥ T_dew + 20°C

smart_warnings.message: Excess air > 30%: major energy waste — each 10% excess air at 250°C stack raises stack loss ≈ 0.6%; install O₂ trim control for continuous optimisation
Stack temperature > 250°C on gas-fired boiler: economiser opportunity — recovering heat to 130°C typically saves 4–8% fuel; verify dew point before sizing
Natural gas boiler below 88% efficiency: condensing technology available — condensing boilers achieve 92–97% (NCV basis) by recovering latent heat of flue gas water vapour
Low O₂ + high CO: sub-stoichiometric combustion — immediate safety risk of CO poisoning and explosion; increase combustion air immediately and check burner condition
Stack loss > 15%: dominant efficiency penalty — target: natural gas boiler stack loss ≤ 10% at 230°C stack; prioritise air preheating or economiser installation

fmea.effect: Boiler efficiency < 75%: critically low — stack temperature and/or excess air are excessive; immediate combustion optimisation and flue gas audit required
CO > 400 ppm: incomplete combustion — adjust air-to-fuel ratio; CO > 1000 ppm indicates safety risk of CO accumulation in boiler room
Stack temperature within 10°C of acid dew point: sulphuric acid condensation risk — economiser and stack lining corrosion; maintain stack temperature ≥ T_dew + 20°C

fmea.failureMode: Excess air > 30%: major energy waste — each 10% excess air at 250°C stack raises stack loss ≈ 0.6%; install O₂ trim control for continuous optimisation
Stack temperature > 250°C on gas-fired boiler: economiser opportunity — recovering heat to 130°C typically saves 4–8% fuel; verify dew point before sizing
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: stack_temp_c > 250 && fuel_type === 'NG'

fmea.failureMode: Natural gas boiler below 88% efficiency: condensing technology available — condensing boilers achieve 92–97% (NCV basis) by recovering latent heat of flue gas water vapour
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: eta_th_pct < 88 && fuel_type === 'NG'

fmea.failureMode: Low O₂ + high CO: sub-stoichiometric combustion — immediate safety risk of CO poisoning and explosion; increase combustion air immediately and check burner condition
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: flue_gas_O2_pct < 1.5 && CO_stack_ppm > 200

fmea.failureMode: Stack loss > 15%: dominant efficiency penalty — target: natural gas boiler stack loss ≤ 10% at 230°C stack; prioritise air preheating or economiser installation
